diagrams.title: K5 - Complete Graph on 5 Vertices
diagrams.description: Shows K5 with 5 vertices and all 10 edges connecting every pair. Demonstrates why it is non-planar - at least 5 crossings are unavoidable.
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof that K5 cannot be drawn without edge crossings - fundamental example of non-planar graph

diagrams.title: K3,3 - Complete Bipartite Graph
diagrams.description: Shows two sets of 3 vertices each with all 9 edges between sets. Demonstrates non-planarity due to unavoidable edge crossings in the middle.
elements: 9 edges connecting every vertex in A to every vertex in B
diagrams.purpose: Shows K3,3 structure and why it's non-planar - complete bipartite structure forces crossings

diagrams.title: Edge Subdivision Example
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the edge subdivision operation: replacing edge (u,v) with vertices u-w-v.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the basic operation used in defining homeomorphic graphs for Kuratowski's Theorem

diagrams.title: Kuratowski's Theorem Summary
diagrams.description: Visual summary showing: Graph is planar ⇔ No K5 or K3,3 subdivisions present
diagrams.purpose: Quick visual reference for applying the theorem to determine planarity
